proposals.statement: Cintas agreed to acquire UniFirst for $310.00 per share in cash and stock, an enterprise value of approximately $5.5 billion, bringing together two family-founded companies.

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: Cintas Corporation (Nasdaq: CTAS) (“Cintas” or the “Company”) and UniFirst Corporation (NYSE: UNF) (“UniFirst”) today announced that they have entered into a definitive agreement under which Cintas will acquire UniFirst for $310.00 per share in cash and stock, representing an enterprise value of approximately $5.5 billion.
proposals.locator: Press release headline para., dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: Under the merger consideration, each UniFirst share is converted into the right to receive $155.00 in cash plus 0.7720 of a share of Cintas common stock; the exchange ratio is fixed and will not be adjusted for changes in either company's stock price.

source.title: UniFirst 424B3 (proxy statement/prospectus)

proposals.quote: each share of UniFirst common stock and UniFirst class B common stock (collectively, “UniFirst stock”) will be converted into the right to receive $155.00 in cash, without interest, and 0.7720 of validly issued, fully paid and non-assessable shares of Cintas common stock (with, if applicable, cash in lieu of fractional shares) (the “merger consideration”), less any applicable withholding taxes.
proposals.locator: Letter to shareholders / Q&A, 'Merger Consideration' (p. 1)

proposals.statement: The fixed exchange ratio implies approximately 14,261,683 Cintas shares issued as stock consideration, leaving former UniFirst shareholders with approximately 3.4% of fully diluted Cintas common stock (as of April 16, 2026).

source.title: UniFirst 424B3 (proxy statement/prospectus)

proposals.quote: the estimated number of shares of Cintas common stock issuable as the stock consideration is approximately 14,261,683 shares, which will result in former UniFirst shareholders holding approximately 3.4% of the outstanding fully diluted Cintas common stock based on the number of outstanding shares of common stock and outstanding long-term incentive awards of Cintas and UniFirst as of April 16, 2026, the most recent practicable date for which such information was available.
proposals.locator: Letter to shareholders / Q&A, stock-consideration discussion (p. 1)

proposals.statement: The $310.00 combined value was based on Cintas' closing share price of $200.77 on March 9, 2026 ($155.00 cash plus 0.7720 x $200.77).

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: This represents a combined value of $310.00 per share based on Cintas’ closing share price of $200.77 on March 9, 2026.
proposals.locator: Press release, 'Transaction Details' section, dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: UniFirst's financial advisors used $153.05 (the closing price on November 11, 2025, the day prior to UniFirst's disclosure that a shareholder had nominated directors to its Board) as the unaffected/undisturbed share price against which the $310.00 merger consideration was compared.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of UniFirst's Financial Advisors

proposals.quote: the unaffected closing share price of UniFirst common stock of $153.05 on November 11, 2025, the day prior to UniFirst’s disclosure that a shareholder had nominated directors to the UniFirst Board and (ii) the value of the merger consideration of $310.00 per share of UniFirst stock.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of J.P. Morgan, Public Trading Multiples (p. 55)

proposals.statement: The $310.00 consideration represents a premium of approximately 102.5% to the proxy's stated unaffected price of $153.05 (310.00 / 153.05 - 1 = 1.025), i.e. roughly a double-up; this headline premium is measured against a Nov 11, 2025 price that itself post-dates Cintas's publicly-disclosed Jan 2025 $275 approach, so the unaffected baseline may already embed takeover speculation.

Cintas asserts it expects to realize approximately $375 million of operating cost synergies — including material cost, production expense, service expense and selling, general and administrative expense — within four years.

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: Cintas expects to benefit from the addition of UniFirst’s talented workforce while also realizing approximately $375 million of operating cost synergies, including material cost, production expense, service expense and selling, general and administrative expense, within four years.
proposals.locator: Press release, 'Unlocks Additional Resources and Cost Synergies' bullet, dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: Cintas states the ~$5.5 billion enterprise value represents a multiple of 8.0x run-rate trailing-twelve-month EBITDA including approximately $375 million of operating cost synergies.

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: The implied total enterprise value of the transaction is approximately $5.5 billion, which represents a multiple of 8.0x run-rate trailing 12 months EBITDA, including approximately $375 million of operating cost synergies.
proposals.locator: Press release, 'Transaction Details' section, dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: Cintas states the transaction is expected to be accretive to Cintas' earnings per share by the end of the second full year after closing.

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: Expected to be accretive to Cintas’ earnings per share by the end of the second full year after closing.
proposals.locator: Press release, transaction-benefits bullets, dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: Cintas states net leverage at close is expected to be 1.5x debt to EBITDA, with the cash consideration funded from cash on hand, committed lines of credit and/or other available financing, and not subject to any financing contingency.

source.title: Cintas to Acquire UniFirst in $5.5 Billion Transaction (press release)

proposals.quote: The cash consideration will be funded with Cintas’ cash on hand, committed lines of credit and/or other available sources of financing, and is not subject to any contingencies.
proposals.locator: Press release, 'Transaction Details' financing para., dated March 11, 2026

proposals.statement: J.P. Morgan's Public Trading Multiples analysis applied a reference range of 8.0x – 10.0x EV/2026E Adjusted EBITDA (Pre-Stock Based Compensation) for UniFirst and indicated an implied per-share equity value range of $147.00 to $182.00.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of J.P. Morgan

proposals.quote: EBITDA (Pre-Stock Based Compensation) $147.00 $182.00 The range of implied per share equity values for UniFirst common stock was compared to (i) the unaffected closing share price of UniFirst common stock of $153.05 on November 11, 2025
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of J.P. Morgan, Public Trading Multiples (p. 55)

proposals.statement: J.P. Morgan's Selected Transactions analysis applied a FV/LTM EBITDA reference range of 11.7x to 14.2x for UniFirst, indicating an implied equity value range of approximately $215.50 to $260.00 per share.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of J.P. Morgan

proposals.quote: The analysis indicated a range of implied equity value per share of UniFirst common stock (rounded to the nearest $0.50) of approximately $215.50 to $260.00, which J.P.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of J.P. Morgan, Selected Transactions Analysis (p. 56)

proposals.statement: J.P. Morgan's Discounted Cash Flow analysis (discount rates 7.50%–8.50%, present value as of February 28, 2026) indicated an implied per-share equity value range of $193.00 to $266.50 for UniFirst common stock.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of J.P. Morgan

proposals.quote: this analysis indicated the following ranges of implied per share equity value (rounded to the nearest $0.50) for UniFirst common stock: Implied Per Share Equity Value Low High UniFirst Discounted Cash Flow $193.00 $266.50
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of J.P. Morgan, Discounted Cash Flow Analysis (p. 57)

proposals.statement: Goldman Sachs' Illustrative Discounted Cash Flow analysis (discount rate 9.0%, equal to its CAPM estimate of UniFirst's cost of equity) resulted in a range of illustrative present values of $160.00 to $218.00 per share of UniFirst stock.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of Goldman Sachs

proposals.quote: This analysis resulted in a range of illustrative present values per share of UniFirst stock of $160.00 to $218.00.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of Goldman Sachs, Illustrative Discounted Cash Flow Analysis (p. 60)

proposals.statement: Goldman Sachs' Selected Precedent Transactions analysis (uniform-services deals since 2015) applied an EV/LTM adjusted EBITDA reference range of 11.7x to 14.2x to UniFirst's LTM adjusted EBITDA as of February 26, 2026, deriving a range of implied per-share values of $216.00 to $260.00.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of Goldman Sachs

proposals.quote: to derive a reference range of implied values per share of UniFirst stock of $216.00 to $260.00.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of Goldman Sachs, Selected Precedent Transactions Analysis (p. 60)

proposals.statement: Goldman Sachs' selected precedent transactions in the uniform-services industry comprised October 2017 Aramark/AmeriPride at 14.2x LTM EV/EBITDA, December 2016 Elis/Lavebras at 11.7x, and August 2016 Cintas/G&K Services at 14.0x.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of Goldman Sachs

proposals.quote: Selected Transactions LTM EV/EBITDA Announcement Date Acquiror Target October 2017 Aramark AmeriPride 14.2x December 2016 Elis Lavebras 11.7x August 2016 Cintas G&K Services 14.0x
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of Goldman Sachs, Selected Precedent Transactions Analysis (p. 60)

proposals.statement: Goldman Sachs' Premia Paid analysis of 568 all-cash U.S. public-target transactions ($1B–$10B EV, Jan 1 2015–Mar 9 2026, excluding finance/real estate) found a median premium of 36%, a 25th-percentile premium of 21% and a 75th-percentile premium of 59% to the target's last undisturbed closing price.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of Goldman Sachs

proposals.quote: This analysis indicated a median premium of 36%, 25th percentile premium of 21% and 75th percentile premium of 59% across the period.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of Goldman Sachs, Premia Paid Analysis (p. 61)

proposals.statement: Applying its 21% to 59% illustrative premium range to the $153.05 undisturbed price (Nov 11, 2025), Goldman Sachs derived implied per-share equity values of $185.00 to $243.00 for UniFirst.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Opinion of Goldman Sachs

proposals.quote: Goldman Sachs applied a reference range of illustrative premiums of 21% to 59% to the undisturbed closing price per share of UniFirst stock of $153.05 as of November 11, 2025 to derive a range of implied equity values per share of UniFirst stock of $185.00 to $243.00.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Opinion of Goldman Sachs, Premia Paid Analysis (p. 61)

proposals.statement: The $310.00 deal price sits above the high end of every standalone-value methodology produced by both advisors (J.P. Morgan: trading-comps $147–$182, DCF $193–$266.50, selected-transactions $215.50–$260; Goldman: DCF $160–$218, precedent-transactions $216–$260, premia-implied $185–$243) — i.e. the price is only reachable on the strength of synergies/control value beyond UniFirst's standalone case.

The ~102.5% headline premium is nearly triple the 36% median premium in Goldman's 568-deal premia-paid sample (102.5 / 36 ≈ 2.85x) and well above its 59% 75th-percentile, underscoring that the premium is an outlier relative to comparable U.S. mid-cap M&A.

Cintas's asserted ~$375M run-rate cost synergies are roughly double UniFirst's ~$184.5M FY2025 operating income (375 / 184.5 ≈ 2.03x), so the entire value case for the ~102.5% premium rests on synergies exceeding the target's standalone operating profit — a heroic-execution flag absent a hard density/cost mechanism.

Cintas's stated 8.0x EV/EBITDA multiple is explicitly computed INCLUDING the ~$375M synergies; backing the synergies out raises the effective ex-synergy entry multiple materially, so the headline 8.0x understates what Cintas pays for UniFirst as-is and the deal economics depend on the synergies actually landing.

Cintas had publicly proposed acquiring UniFirst for $275.00 per share (all cash, no financing condition) in a January 7, 2025 press release after private approaches in November–December 2024, and the UniFirst Board unanimously concluded that proposal was not in the best interests of UniFirst and its shareholders.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Background of the Mergers

proposals.quote: Following the meeting, at the direction of the UniFirst Board, UniFirst delivered a letter to Cintas reiterating that the UniFirst Board carefully considered Cintas’ $275.00 per share proposal and unanimously concluded that the proposal was not in the best interests of UniFirst, its shareholders, and other stakeholders.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Background of the Mergers (negotiation history)

proposals.statement: During negotiations Cintas's advisor Morgan Stanley characterized $275.00 per share as a 'full and fair price' and a 'very full offer', stated $275.00 was the maximum Cintas would deliver in cash, and noted any additional consideration would be delivered in Cintas equity — explaining the cash-plus-stock structure of the final $310 deal.

source.title: UniFirst DEFM14A — Background of the Mergers

proposals.quote: They further noted that $275.00 per share was the maximum value that Cintas would want to deliver in cash; any additional consideration would be delivered in Cintas equity.
proposals.locator: The Mergers — Background of the Mergers (Jan 2026 negotiations)
